_instructions: Copy this file, rename it persona.json, and fill in your values. This file is referenced by the n8n workflows -- the voice_brief, content_folders, and caption_style sections are what you paste into the workflow nodes.

persona.name: your_handle
persona.voice_brief: Replace this with a detailed description of your content voice. The AI writes every caption in exactly this voice. Be specific: describe your tone (warm/bold/funny/mysterious), what you talk about, what you never say, and what your audience expects from you. The more detail you give, the more on-brand the captions will be. Example: 'Confident, warm, slightly playful. Never corporate, never thirsty. Short punchy captions. She states, she invites, she disappears. Hashtags go in first comment only, never in the caption body.'

platforms._note: x_manual means captions are generated but you post to X yourself (API write access costs $100/month). Set instagram and threads to false if you don't use them.

content_folders._note: Each key is a subfolder name inside your CONTENT_BASE folder. blur levels: aggressive (pole/dance/skin), moderate (outfits/activewear), light (mild content), none (fully safe). vault_only content is saved to your vault and never posted.

caption_style._note_hashtags: Hashtags go in the first comment so they don't clutter the caption. Set hashtags_in_first_comment to false to put them at the bottom of the caption instead.

suggested_post_times._note: Best time to post for each folder type. Used in the WhatsApp approval message as a suggestion.

paths._note: These should match your environment variables. Set them in Windows Machine env vars via PowerShell (see docs/credentials-setup.md).
